metadata.summary: Qualys SSL Labs API in Python

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#hpkppolicy

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.trust.TrustData.isTrusted

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.trust.TrustData.trustErrorMessage

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.hsts_preload.HstsPreloadData.status

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.hsts_preload.HstsPreloadData.error

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#hstspreload

classes.name: ssllabs.ssllabs.Ssllabs

methods.name: ssllabs.ssllabs.Ssllabs.availability
documentation:
Check the availability of the SSL Labs servers.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#error-response-status-codes

methods.name: ssllabs.ssllabs.Ssllabs.analyze
documentation:
Test a particular host with respect to the cool off and the maximum number of assessments.

```
param host

Host to test

param publish

True if assessment results should be published on the public results boards

param ignore_mismatch

True if assessment shall proceed even when the server certificate doesn't match the hostname

param from_cache

True if cached results should be used instead of new assessments

param max_age

Maximum age cached data might have in hours
```

See also: <https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#protocol-usage>

methods.name: ssllabs.ssllabs.Ssllabs.info
documentation:
Retrieve the engine and criteria version, and initialize the maximum number of concurrent assessments.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#info

methods.name: ssllabs.ssllabs.Ssllabs.root_certs
documentation:
Retrieve root certificates.

```
param trust_store

Trust store to return (1-Mozilla, 2-Apple MacOS, 3-Android, 4-Java, 5-Windows)
```

See also: <https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#retrieve-root-certificates>

methods.name: ssllabs.ssllabs.Ssllabs.status_codes
documentation:
Retrieve known status codes.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#retrieve-known-status-codes
Highlevel methods to interact with the SSL Labs Assessment APIs.

classes.name: ssllabs.data.caa_record.CaaRecordData

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.caa_record.CaaRecordData.tag
documentation: A property of the CAA record

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.caa_record.CaaRecordData.value
documentation: Corresponding value of a CAA property

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.caa_record.CaaRecordData.flags
documentation: Corresponding flags of CAA property (8 bit)

Dataclass for CAA Record objects.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#caarecord

classes.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.host
documentation: Assessment host, which can be a hostname or an IP address

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.port
documentation: Assessment port (e.g., 443)

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.protocol
documentation: Protocol (e.g., HTTP)

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.isPublic
documentation: True if this assessment is publicly available (listed on the SSL Labs assessment boards)

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.status
documentation: Assessment status; possible values: DNS, ERROR, IN_PROGRESS, and READY.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.statusMessage
documentation: Status message in English. When status is ERROR, this field will contain an error message.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.startTime
documentation: Assessment starting time, in milliseconds since 1970

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.testTime
documentation: Assessment completion time, in milliseconds since 1970

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.engineVersion
documentation: Assessment engine version (e.g., '1.26.5')

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.criteriaVersion
documentation: Grading criteria version (e.g., '2009l')

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.cacheExpiryTime
documentation:
When will the assessment results expire from the cache (typically set only for assessment with errors; otherwise the
results stay in the cache for as long as there's sufficient room)

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.certHostnames
documentation:
The list of certificate hostnames collected from the certificates seen during assessment. The hostnames may not be valid.
This field is available only if the server certificate doesn't match the requested hostname. In that case, this field
saves you some time as you don't have to inspect the certificates yourself to find out what valid hostnames might be.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.endpoints
documentation: List of Endpoint objects

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.host.HostData.certs
documentation: A list of Cert object, representing the chain certificates in the order in which they were retrieved from the server.

Dataclass for host objects.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#host

classes.name: ssllabs.data.protocol_suites.ProtocolSuitesData

documentation: List of Suite objects

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.protocol_suites.ProtocolSuitesData.preference
documentation: True if the server actively selects cipher suites; if null, we were not able to determine if the server has a preference

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.protocol_suites.ProtocolSuitesData.chaCha20Preference
documentation:
True if the server takes into account client preferences when deciding if to use ChaCha20 suites. null, we were not able
to determine if the server has a chacha preference.

Dataclass for protocol suites objects.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#protocolsuites

documentation:
A map containing all status details codes and the corresponding English translations. Please note that, once in use, the
codes will not change, whereas the translations may change at any time.

Dataclass for StatusCodes instances.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#statuscodes

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.simulation.SimulationData.dhG
documentation: DH params, g component

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.simulation.SimulationData.dhYs

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.simulation.SimulationData.keyAlg

List of IDs of each certificate, representing the chain certificates in the order in which they were retrieved from the

documentation: Protocol version, e.g. 771 for TLS 1.2 (0x0303)

documentation: Protocol version, e.g. 1.2, 1.1 etc

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.protocol.ProtocolData.v2SuitesDisabled

Some servers have SSLv2 protocol enabled, but with all SSLv2 cipher suites disabled. In that case, this field is set to

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#protocol

documentation: Certificate ID

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.subject
documentation: Certificate subject

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.commonNames
documentation: Common names extracted from the subject

documentation: Timestamp before which the certificate is not valid (Unix Timestamp)

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.notAfter

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.issuerSubject
documentation: Issuer subject

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.sigAlg
documentation: Certificate signature algorithm

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.revocationInfo

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.crlURIs

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.ocspURIs
documentation: OCSP URIs extracted from the certificate

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.revocationStatus
documentation: A number that describes the revocation status of the certificate

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.crlRevocationStatus

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.ocspRevocationStatus
documentation: Same as revocationStatus, but only for the OCSP information (if any).

documentation: True if CAA is supported else false.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.caaPolicy
documentation: CAA Policy, Null if CAA is not supported

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.mustStaple
documentation: True if stapling is supported else false

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.sgc
documentation: Server Gated Cryptography support

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.validationType
documentation: E for Extended Validation certificates

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.issues
documentation: list of certificate issues, one bit per issue

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.sct
documentation: True if the certificate contains an embedded SCT; false otherwise.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.sha1Hash
documentation: sha1 hash of the certificate

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.sha256Hash
documentation: sha256 hash of the certificate

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.pinSha256
documentation: sha256 hash of the public key

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.keyAlg
documentation: Key algorithm.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.keySize
documentation: Key size, in bits appropriate for the key algorithm.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.keyStrength
documentation: Key strength, in equivalent RSA bits

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.keyKnownDebianInsecure
documentation: True if debian flaw is found, else false

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.cert.CertData.raw
documentation: PEM-encoded certificate

Dataclass for Cert objects.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#cert

classes.name: ssllabs.data.info.InfoData

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.info.InfoData.engineVersion
documentation: SSL Labs software version as a string (e.g., '1.11.14')

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.info.InfoData.criteriaVersion
documentation: Rating criteria version as a string (e.g., '2009f')

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.info.InfoData.maxAssessments
documentation: The maximum number of concurrent assessments the client is allowed to initiate.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.info.InfoData.currentAssessments
documentation: The number of ongoing assessments submitted by this client.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.info.InfoData.newAssessmentCoolOff
documentation:
The cool-off period after each new assessment, in milliseconds; you're not allowed to submit a new assessment before
the cool-off expires, otherwise you'll get a 429.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.info.InfoData.messages
documentation:
A list of messages (strings). Messages can be public (sent to everyone) and private (sent only to the invoking client).
Private messages are prefixed with '[Private]'.

Dataclass for info objects.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#info

classes.name: ssllabs.data.http_transaction.HttpTransactionData

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.http_transaction.HttpTransactionData.requestUrl
documentation: Request URL

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.http_transaction.HttpTransactionData.statusCode
documentation: Response status code

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.http_transaction.HttpTransactionData.requestLine
documentation: The entire request line as a single field

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.http_transaction.HttpTransactionData.requestHeaders
documentation: An array of request HTTP headers, each with name and value

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.http_transaction.HttpTransactionData.responseLine
documentation: The entire response line as a single field

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.http_transaction.HttpTransactionData.responseHeadersRaw
documentation: All response headers as a single field (useful if the headers are malformed)

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.http_transaction.HttpTransactionData.responseHeaders
documentation: An array of response HTTP headers, each with name and value

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.http_transaction.HttpTransactionData.fragileServer
documentation: True if the server crashes when inspected by SSL Labs (in which case the full test is refused)

Dataclass for HTTP Transaction objects.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#httptransaction

classes.name: ssllabs.data.sim_details.SimDetailsData

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.sim_details.SimDetailsData.results
documentation: Instances of Simulation.

Dataclass for Simulation object lists.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#simdetails

classes.name: ssllabs.data.sim_client.SimClientData

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.sim_client.SimClientData.id
documentation: Unique client ID.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.sim_client.SimClientData.name
documentation: Name of the client (e.g., Chrome).

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.sim_client.SimClientData.platform
documentation: Name of the platform (e.g., XP SP3).

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.sim_client.SimClientData.version
documentation: Version of the software being simulated (e.g., 49)

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.sim_client.SimClientData.isReference
documentation:
True if the browser is considered representative of modern browsers, false otherwise. This flag does not correlate to
client's capabilities, but is used by SSL Labs to determine if a particular configuration is effective. For example, to
track Forward Secrecy support, we mark several representative browsers as "modern" and then test to see if they succeed in
negotiating a FS suite. Just as an illustration, modern browsers are currently Chrome, Firefox (not ESR versions),
IE/Win7, and Safari.

Dataclass for Simulation Client objects.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#simclient

classes.name: ssllabs.data.static_pkp_policy.StaticPkpPolicyData

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.static_pkp_policy.StaticPkpPolicyData.status
documentation: SPKP status

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.static_pkp_policy.StaticPkpPolicyData.error
documentation: Error message, when the policy is invalid

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.static_pkp_policy.StaticPkpPolicyData.includeSubDomains
documentation: True if the includeSubDomains directive is set else false

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.static_pkp_policy.StaticPkpPolicyData.reportUri

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.static_pkp_policy.StaticPkpPolicyData.pins

classes.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.hostStartTime
documentation:
Endpoint assessment starting time, in milliseconds since 1970. This field is useful when test results are retrieved in
several HTTP invocations. Then, you should check that the hostStartTime value matches the startTime value of the host.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.certChains
documentation: Server Certificate chains

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.protocols
documentation: Supported protocols

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.suites
documentation: Supported cipher suites per protocol

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.noSniSuites
documentation: Cipher suites observed only with client that does not support Server Name Indication (SNI).

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.namedGroups
documentation: Instance of NamedGroups object.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.serverSignature
documentation:
Contents of the HTTP Server response header when known. This field could be absent for one of two reasons: 1) the HTTP
request failed (check httpStatusCode) or 2) there was no Server response header returned.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.prefixDelegation
documentation: True if this endpoint is reachable via a hostname with the www prefix

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.nonPrefixDelegation
documentation: True if this endpoint is reachable via a hostname without the www prefix

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.vulnBeast
documentation: True if the endpoint is vulnerable to the BEAST attack

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.renegSupport
documentation: This is an integer value that describes the endpoint support for renegotiation.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.sessionResumption
documentation: This is an integer value that describes endpoint support for session resumption.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.compressionMethods
documentation: Integer value that describes supported compression methods

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.supportsNpn
documentation: True if the server supports NPN

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.npnProtocols
documentation: Space separated list of supported NPN protocols

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.supportsAlpn
documentation: True if the server supports ALPN

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.alpnProtocols
documentation: Space separated list of supported ALPN protocols

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.sessionTickets
documentation: Indicates support for Session Tickets

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.ocspStapling
documentation: True if OCSP stapling is deployed on the server

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.staplingRevocationStatus
documentation: Same as Cert.revocationStatus, but for the stapled OCSP response.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.staplingRevocationErrorMessage
documentation: Description of the problem with the stapled OCSP response, if any.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.sniRequired
documentation: If SNI support is required to access the web site.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.httpStatusCode
documentation:
Status code of the final HTTP response seen. When submitting HTTP requests, redirections are followed, but only if they
lead to the same hostname. If this field is not available, that means the HTTP request failed.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.httpForwarding
documentation: Available on a server that responded with a redirection to some other hostname.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.supportsRc4
documentation: True if the server supports at least one RC4 suite.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.rc4WithModern
documentation: True if RC4 is used with modern clients.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.rc4Only
documentation: True if only RC4 suites are supported.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.forwardSecrecy
documentation: Indicates support for Forward Secrecy

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.supportsAead
documentation: True if the server supports at least one AEAD suite.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.supportsCBC
documentation: True if the server supports at least one CBC suite.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.protocolIntolerance
documentation: Indicates protocol version intolerance issues

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.miscIntolerance
documentation: Indicates various other types of intolerance

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.sims
documentation: Instance of SimDetails.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.heartbleed
documentation: True if the server is vulnerable to the Heartbleed attack.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.heartbeat
documentation: True if the server supports the Heartbeat extension.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.openSslCcs
documentation: Results of the CVE-2014-0224 test

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.openSSLLuckyMinus20
documentation: Results of the CVE-2016-2107 test

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.ticketbleed
documentation: Results of the ticketbleed CVE-2016-9244 test

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.bleichenbacher
documentation: Results of the Return Of Bleichenbacher's Oracle Threat (ROBOT) test

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.zombiePoodle
documentation: Results of the Zombie POODLE test

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.goldenDoodle
documentation: Results of the GOLDENDOODLE test

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.zeroLengthPaddingOracle
documentation: Results of the 0-Length Padding Oracle (CVE-2019-1559) test

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.sleepingPoodle
documentation: Results of the Sleeping POODLE test

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.poodle
documentation: True if the endpoint is vulnerable to POODLE

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.poodleTls
documentation: Results of the POODLE TLS test

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.fallbackScsv
documentation:
True if the server supports TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV, false if it doesn't. This field will not be available if the server's
support for TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV can't be tested because it supports only one protocol version (e.g., only TLS 1.2).

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.freak
documentation: True if the server is vulnerable to the FREAK attack, meaning it supports 512-bit key exchange.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.hasSct
documentation: Information about the availability of certificate transparency information (embedded SCTs)

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.dhPrimes
documentation: List of hex-encoded DH primes used by the server. Not present if the server doesn't support the DH key exchange.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.dhUsesKnownPrimes
documentation: Whether the server uses known DH primes. Not present if the server doesn't support the DH key exchange.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.dhYsReuse
documentation: True if the DH ephemeral server value is reused. Not present if the server doesn't support the DH key exchange.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.ecdhParameterReuse
documentation: True if the server reuses its ECDHE values

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.logjam
documentation: True if the server uses DH parameters weaker than 1024 bits.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.chaCha20Preference
documentation:
True if the server takes into account client preferences when deciding if to use ChaCha20 suites. Will be deprecated in
new version.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.hstsPolicy
documentation: Server's HSTS policy. Experimental.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.hstsPreloads
documentation: Information about preloaded HSTS policies.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.hpkpPolicy
documentation: Server's HPKP policy.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.hpkpRoPolicy
documentation: Server's HPKP-RO policy.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.staticPkpPolicy
documentation: Server's SPKP policy.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.httpTransactions
documentation: An array of HttpTransaction objects.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.drownHosts
documentation: List of DROWN hosts.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.drownErrors
documentation: True if error occurred in the DROWN test.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.drownVulnerable
documentation: True if server vulnerable to the DROWN attack.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.implementsTLS13MandatoryCS
documentation: True if server supports mandatory TLS 1.3 cipher suite (TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256), null if TLS 1.3 not supported.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint_details.EndpointDetailsData.zeroRTTEnabled
documentation: Results of the 0-RTT test. This test will only be performed if TLS 1.3 is enabled.

Dataclass for endpoint detail objects.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#endpointdetails

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.ipAddress
documentation: Endpoint IP address, in IPv4 or IPv6 format.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.serverName
documentation: Server name retrieved via reverse DNS

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.statusMessage
documentation: Assessment status message; this field will contain 'Ready' if the endpoint assessment was successful.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.statusDetails
documentation: Code of the operation currently in progress

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.statusDetailsMessage
documentation: Description of the operation currently in progress

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.grade
documentation: Possible values: A+, A-, A-F, T (no trust) and M (certificate name mismatch)

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.gradeTrustIgnored
documentation: Grade (as above), if trust issues are ignored

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.futureGrade
documentation: Next grade because of upcoming grading criteria changes, Null if there is no impact on current grade.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.hasWarnings
documentation: If this endpoint has warnings that might affect the score (e.g., get A- instead of A).

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.isExceptional
documentation: This flag will be raised when an exceptional configuration is encountered. The SSL Labs test will give such sites an A+.

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.progress
documentation: Assessment progress, which is a value from 0 to 100, and -1 if the assessment has not yet started

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.duration
documentation: Assessment duration, in milliseconds

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.eta
documentation: Estimated time, in seconds, until the completion of the assessment

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.delegation
documentation: Indicates domain name delegation with and without the www prefix

class_variables.name: ssllabs.data.endpoint.EndpointData.details
documentation:
This field contains an EndpointDetails object. It's not present by default, but can be enabled by using the "all"
parameter to the analyze API call.

classes.name: ssllabs.api.analyze.Analyze

methods.name: ssllabs.api.analyze.Analyze.get
documentation:
Analyze host.

```
param host

Hostname to analyze

key publish

Set to "on" if assessment results should be published on the public results boards; optional, defaults
to "off".

key startNew

If set to "on" then cached assessment results are ignored and a new assessment is started. However,
if there's already an assessment in progress, its status is delivered instead. This parameter should be
used only once to initiate a new assessment; further invocations should omit it to avoid causing an
assessment loop.

key fromCache

Always deliver cached assessment reports if available; optional, defaults to "off". This parameter is
intended for API consumers that don't want to wait for assessment results. Can't be used at the same
time as the startNew parameter.

key maxAge

Maximum report age, in hours, if retrieving from cache (fromCache parameter set).

key all

By default this call results only summaries of individual endpoints. If this parameter is set to "on", full
information will be returned. If set to "done", full information will be returned only if the assessment is
complete (status is READY or ERROR).

key ignoreMismatch

Set to "on" to proceed with assessments even when the server certificate doesn't match the
assessment hostname. Set to off by default. Please note that this parameter is ignored if a
cached report is returned.

raises httpx.ConnectTimeout

SSL Labs Servers don't respond.

raises httpx.HTTPStatusError

A client or server error response occured.

raises httpx.ReadTimeout

SSL Labs Servers don't respond.
```

Invoke assessment and check progress.

See also: https://github.com/ssllabs/ssllabs-scan/blob/master/ssllabs-api-docs-v3.md#invoke-assessment-and-check-progress
